title: Lazy Sunday Ethiopian Dried White Corn Pilaf
description: A salty and velvety Ethiopian dish featuring dried white corn, perfect for a lazy Sunday, made with halal-friendly ingredients and cooked to perfection in an air fryer.
instructions: Preheat the air fryer to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the soaked and drained dried white corn, chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger.
In a small bowl, whisk together the vegetable oil, salt, black pepper, turmeric, and paprika.
Pour the oil mixture over the corn mixture and stir until the corn is well coated.
Add the chicken broth to the bowl and stir until the corn is submerged.
Transfer the corn mixture to the air fryer basket and cook for 20 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes.
After 20 minutes, reduce the air fryer temperature to 300 degrees Fahrenheit and continue cooking for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the pilaf from the air fryer and stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
